name: office365-blade-mcp
title: Microsoft Office 365
description: Microsoft 365 operations via Graph API — email, calendar, tasks (To Do + Planner). Token-efficient, write-gated, dual auth.
tagline: Email, calendar, and tasks through Microsoft Graph
author: groupthink-dev

env.name: O365_CLIENT_SECRET
env.description: Azure AD app client secret (optional — for client_credentials flow)

env.name: O365_WRITE_ENABLED
env.description: Enable write operations (send, reply, create, update, delete)

setup.help_text: 1. Register an app at Azure Portal → App registrations → New registration. 2. Set platform to 'Mobile and desktop applications' for device code flow. 3. Copy the Tenant ID and Client ID. 4. On first run, complete the device code auth flow in your browser.
setup.blurb: Connect to Microsoft 365 for email, calendar, and task management through a single MCP server.

fields.label: Auth Mode
fields.help: Use 'device_code' for interactive setup, 'client_credentials' for headless/CI

prerequisites: Azure AD app registration with Microsoft Graph API permissions
API permissions: Mail.ReadWrite, Mail.Send, Calendars.ReadWrite, Tasks.ReadWrite

highlights.text: Email — inbox, search, threads, send, reply, folders
Calendar — events, free/busy, today/week views, RSVP
Tasks — To Do lists, Planner tasks, create, complete
Write-gated — reads work immediately, writes need explicit opt-in
Dual auth — device code for interactive, client credentials for headless
Token-efficient — concise formatters strip Graph API verbosity

readme:
### What it does

Office 365 Blade MCP gives your AI agent access to Microsoft 365 email, calendar, and tasks through the Microsoft Graph API. One MCP server covers three service contracts — `email-v1`, `calendar-v1`, and `tasks-v1` — so you can triage your inbox, check your schedule, and manage your task list in a single conversation.

Your agent can read and search email, send replies, browse calendar events, check free/busy slots, create meetings, and manage To Do and Planner tasks.

### Token efficiency

Microsoft Graph API responses are notoriously large — a single email message can return 50+ fields including internal routing headers, change keys, and OData metadata. Each tool includes a concise formatter that extracts subject, sender, date, body preview, and flags — dropping everything else. This keeps context windows clean across sessions with dozens of API calls.

### Safety model

All operations follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — inbox, search, events, tasks, free/busy
- **Writes** require `O365_WRITE_ENABLED=true` — send, reply, create event, update task
- **Destructive operations** are double-gated — delete email, cancel events

### Authentication

Two auth modes are supported:

- **Device code flow** (default) — interactive setup, user authenticates in the browser
- **Client credentials flow** — headless/CI environments with a client secret

Both require an Azure AD app registration with appropriate Graph API permissions.

scenarios.label: Morning email and calendar review
scenarios.body: Pull today's calendar events and inbox messages, identify scheduling conflicts, flag emails that need replies, and summarise the day's commitments.

scenarios.label: Convert emails to tasks
scenarios.body: Search the inbox for action items, create To Do tasks from relevant messages, move processed emails to an archive folder, and verify the task list reflects the new items.

scenarios.label: Schedule a meeting across attendees
scenarios.body: Check free/busy availability for multiple attendees, find the first open slot that works for everyone, create the calendar event, and confirm the invitation was sent.
